goal: Speed up mainline sync: combine the three sequential 'git fetch origin' calls (main, actor logs, notes) into a single fetch with multiple refspecs — ~9s → ~3s wall time on a real remote because each fetch costs one ssh handshake

summary.title: Sync: combine 3 fetches into 1 — ~10s → ~4s
summary.what: Service.Sync used to call s.Git.Fetch three times sequentially (main, actor logs, notes). Each fetch is a separate ssh handshake and round-trip to the remote (~3s on github). Replaced with a single Fetch passing all three refspecs at once, so one ssh session covers everything.
summary.why: Measured: 10.2s → 3.9s wall on this repo. Sync is the most-called interactive command (status, log, reconcile all rely on a fresh sync); shaving 6s off it directly improves the inner loop. The change is also simpler — Git.Fetch already accepts variadic refspecs.
summary.user_goal: Make mainline sync feel snappy in real usage instead of the 10s pause that has been adding up across the dogfood session.

decisions.chose: Single git fetch with three refspecs
decisions.rationale: git fetch trivially accepts multiple refspecs and runs them in one wire round-trip; parallel fetches against the same .git would race on the lock.
rejected: Three goroutines each calling git fetch — concurrent access to .git is not guaranteed safe; refs/objects writes can collide.
Tell users to set up ssh ControlMaster — pushes the optimisation outside mainline's control.

risks: If any of the three refspecs fails the whole fetch returns error; previously a failure on (e.g.) notes would not have blocked main fetch. Mitigated because Sync ignored the per-call error before too — they were swallowed silently. Same observable behaviour, different combined error path.
followups: Measure on a fresh clone with many actor refs — refspec count scales with team size, may hit a different bottleneck.
Consider partial-clone / fetch --filter for very large mainline histories.

architectural_claims: Sync issues a single git fetch per remote, regardless of how many ref namespaces it cares about.
behavioral_changes: mainline sync wall time on a real ssh remote drops from ~10s to ~4s.
